articles.title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
articles.description:
Seamark said the decision was due to "economic challenges." The labor union thinks the decision was retaliatory.

The post Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Seamark at Encore Boston will close due to economic challenges on March 11, 2023.
Workers unionized less than a month before the closure, raising concerns of retaliation.
The closure affects local employment and dining options for Boston residents.

articles.title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
articles.description:
Citing falling enrollment and growing costs, the St. Joseph School will close at the end of the school year.

The post A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: St. Joseph School in Medford will close due to enrollment decline and rising costs.
The school has served the community for nearly 100 years, impacting local families.
Parents must seek alternative education options for their children after the school year ends.

articles.title: This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health
articles.description:
Chris Ruggio is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital Miles for Miracles team.

The post This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Chris Ruggio will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital.
Ruggio is a 14-time marathoner raising funds for children's health.
His efforts support local families and improve healthcare access in Boston.

articles.title: A 15-year-old girl was found dead in a Plymouth forest in 1986. Her alleged killer is finally standing trial.
articles.description:
Michael Hand is accused of dropping a 73-pound rock on Tracy Gilpin’s head and leaving her body in Myles Standish State Forest.

The post A 15-year-old girl was found dead in a Plymouth forest in 1986. Her alleged killer is finally standing trial. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Michael Hand is on trial for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin.
Hand allegedly killed Gilpin by dropping a 73-pound rock on her head.
This trial may bring closure to Gilpin's family and the Plymouth community.

articles.title: Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds for each Mass. household
articles.description:
All told, Gov. Maura Healey says that the government owes Massachusetts residents more than $4.8 billion.

The post Healey demands $1,745 tariff refunds for each Mass. household appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Governor Maura Healey demands $1,745 refunds for each Massachusetts household.
Massachusetts residents are owed over $4.8 billion in total tariff refunds.
Refunds aim to alleviate financial burden on local families amid rising costs.

articles.title: Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme
articles.description:
Federal prosecutors say the Boston retailer exchanged food assistance benefits for cash and ineligible items over several years.

The post Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mattapan store owner pleaded guilty to $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme.
Owner exchanged food assistance benefits for cash and ineligible items over several years.
Local residents may face reduced access to legitimate food assistance programs.
